4,245

Adds studies/conferences, broader disease terms, secondary indications (HF, CKD), risk factors, OTC/supplements.

Side effects (–100) excluded from all three tiers. 1,591 keywords flagged for exclusion lists and adverse event monitoring only.

Quality flag: 138 keywords mention UTI or yeast infection but scored positive These are long question-format keywords that contain both a T2D term (e.g. "type 2 diabetic") and a side effect mention (e.g. "uti" or "yeast infection"). Because rules are first-match-wins, the T2D pattern fires before the side effect pattern. All 138 represent T2D patients whose question happened to reference a side effect in the same sentence. They are technically in the T2D audience but may be currently experiencing a Jardiance side effect. Review before including in acquisition targeting.

19 year old married. fish smell. treated it like a yeast infection monistat. despite no thick discharge or itch. std free.smell isn't going away. help?
33 weeks preg and i have a yeast infection! my doctor prescribed one pill of the fluconazole! read on internet that can cause birth defects! true ?
33 weeks pregnant/ terrible yeast infection. dr prescribed diflucan. i took 2 150mg at 8 weeks preg. this dose is another 150 mg. is this safe to take?
36 wks preg, yeast infection, dilated a fingertip. lost mucus plug 2 wks ago. baby still safe from infection? can i treat the yeast w/ otc cream?
36 wks pregnant. dilated a fingertip, cervix thinning. have yeast infection. is clotrimazole cream still safe to use while dilated?
37 weeks pregnant with yeast infection, nurse told me to use monistat 1, (tioconazole) never used before is it safe when i am also having discharges/fluid leakage?
after 1g of taking azithromycin when can ii take diflucan (fluconazole) because a week after i got a yeast infection?
after having a yeast infection my clitoris has become very sensitive to the touch, it has been going on for about a week or so now, is this normal?
after iud (copper) i am having yeast infection repeatedly! have been taking duflocan twice a week and infection cream external and internal .
after taking monistat 3, when i pee white clumps come out. is this normal and does it mean its working? is this the products coming out or the yeast infection?
after two weeks of taking doxycycline hyc for chlamydia is it possible to get a yeast infection on the last day of taking it?
amoxicillin caused yeast infection. still have 3 more days. do i need to switch to a new antibiotic or can i just use monistat?

are swollen glands be linked to a oral yeast infection?
been taking flagyl and keflex for diverticulitis have developed a yeast infection, can i take fluconazole along with my antibiotics to treat it?
been treating myself w terazol (terconazole) for 4 days now - lab results just came. no yeast infection. should i continue treatment or stop? will it mess up my ph?
being treated for a yeast infection on arm. can i sleep in the same bed as my wife?
boyfriend ate me out while i had a yeast infection. should i tell him?

can a baby with diaper rash or yeast infection go swimming?

can a chronic yeast infection be caused by an uncircumcised sex partner?

Deliberately Zero-Scored Terms

Terms present in the keyword file explicitly scored 0 during rule-building — disease-adjacent but too non-specific to use as targeting signals.

back pain — No meaningful T2D specificity
blurred vision — Real T2D symptom but too non-specific — also glaucoma, migraines, MS, and dozens of other conditions
dry mouth — Non-specific; common side effect of many unrelated medications
extreme fatigue — Common across hundreds of conditions; not an actionable disease-specific signal
fatigue — Far too non-specific; not usable as a T2D targeting signal
frequent infections — Too vague; not actionable as a T2D-specific signal
headache — No meaningful T2D specificity
nausea — Non-specific; much more strongly associated with GLP-1 agonist side effects than T2D itself
thirst — Without 'excessive' qualifier, too non-specific for targeting
weight loss — Could be intentional weight management or unintentional disease symptom; too ambiguous

Keywords to Use — Operations Team

Keywords to Use

Shortest representative keyword per match-reason group. Sorted by score descending. OTC terms excluded pending confirmation. Cutoff: Max Avails (1+).
